title: Correctness, Completeness, Optimality, Metrics, and Selection · Program Defense
summary: A release reviewer compares near-miss decisions for correctness, completeness, optimality, metrics, and selection and accepts only runnable changed-case evidence, explicit limitations, and named browser, Tcl, Tk, display, input, assistive-technology, file, package, and production transfer gates. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for solver benchmark and decision report. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging,…
objectives: Validate endpoints, adjacency, passability, uniqueness policy, and reported cost against an independent route checker.
State the finite graph and resource assumptions under which a solver will find a path if one exists.
Tie shortest or least-cost claims to edge weights, heuristic properties, termination rule, and verified route cost.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-maze-solver-python cumulative accessible desktop maze portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python grid, graph, search, state, and scheduler models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts,
focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, display scaling,
packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazePolicy:
    rows: int = 12
    columns: int = 16
    seed: int = 314159
    maximum_cells: int = 16_384
    reduced_motion: bool = False


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazeEvidence:
    revision: str
    topology_digest: str
    action_trace_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str
    artifact_digest: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python maze solver portfolio ready"

steps.title: Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Correctness Witness · changed case · Quiz Correctness Witness
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz puzzle team handles case 2277 during a corrupted-state recovery drill. Build deterministic Python evidence to compare solver correctness, completeness, optimality, work, memory, and selection on identical fixtures; the player cannot rely on color or animation; then change display scale and retain focus and control access. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, and packaged desktop behavior require explicit controlled native transfer gates. steps.instruction:
Write the quiz evidence note for Solver Selection: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A quiz puzzle team handles case 2002 during a museum kiosk accessibility review. Build deterministic Python evidence to compare solver correctness, completeness, optimality, work, memory, and selection on identical fixtures; all graph claims need an independent witness; then change display scale and retain focus and control access. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only.
